prompt:
You are Agent 5 of 5 working on a codebase at /var/folders/45/myq5db_n6q5cyhtb39vkbmp80000gn/T/twining-bench-ZsfvlI.

## Codebase Orientation
This is a TypeScript service. Previous sessions built the full analytics feature. Check these locations:
- `src/routes/` — API route handlers (connected in Session 4)
- `src/services/` — aggregation service implementation (Session 2)
- `src/models/` or `src/types/` — analytics data model definitions (Session 1)
- `tests/` — existing unit tests (Session 3)

Your task: Write integration tests for the full analytics pipeline and ensure everything works end-to-end.

Specifically:
1. Review the full analytics stack in `src/`: routes → handlers → aggregation service → data models.
2. Write integration tests that test the full pipeline from HTTP request to response.
3. Test key scenarios: fetching dashboard data, querying specific metrics, time-range filtering.
4. Test error scenarios: invalid parameters, missing data, unauthorized access.
5. Fix any integration issues you discover.
6. Run all tests (unit and integration) and ensure they pass.

Important:
- This is the final session — everything should work end-to-end.
- Fix integration issues but don't restructure — keep changes minimal.
- Make sure ALL tests (unit and integration) pass when you're done.

parameters.task: Write integration tests for the full analytics pipeline (routes → handlers → aggregation service → data models) and fix any integration issues

parameters.summary: Integration tests exercise full pipeline: RouteRequest → AnalyticsRoutes → AnalyticsService → Repositories → Database
parameters.context: Sessions 1-4 built the analytics feature (models, service, unit tests, routes). Integration tests were needed to verify end-to-end correctness across all layers.
parameters.rationale: Follow established test pattern: real Database/Repository instances, no mocks. Tests cover all 6 route handlers with seeded data, validate cross-endpoint consistency (dashboard metrics match standalone endpoints), error scenarios (invalid params, missing required fields), and data filtering. 31 integration tests added.

alternatives.option: Use supertest with an HTTP server layer
alternatives.reason_rejected: Project has zero runtime dependencies and uses framework-agnostic route handlers — no HTTP server to test against

alternatives.option: Mock the AnalyticsService in integration tests
alternatives.reason_rejected: Defeats the purpose of integration testing; existing codebase pattern uses real instances

parameters.summary: Agent 5: Added 31 integration tests for full analytics pipeline — all 159 tests pass, TypeScript clean
parameters.detail: Created tests/integration/analytics-pipeline.integration.test.ts with 31 tests covering: dashboard end-to-end (complete data, empty state, granularity, date range, topProductsLimit), order metrics (correct computation, live repository changes), user metrics (role breakdown), top products (ranking, limiting), time series (day/month aggregation, date filtering), order metrics for period (date range, empty results), error handling (11 error scenarios: invalid granularity, dates, limits, missing required params), and cross-endpoint consistency (5 tests verifying dashboard sub-results match standalone endpoints). No integration issues discovered — all layers work correctly together. Full suite: 159/159 passing.
